mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based domestic demand expansion, which increases corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in both series fails to yield a positive return frequency above 58% or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3% annually or 5-day forward returns show no significant deviation from zero during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 2.5% annually triggers expectations of monetary tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in greater than 65% of high-inflation observations or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return frequency above 60% or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for GDP growth revisions.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels reflect stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue growth, which directly drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently fail to predict 20-day return direction with a Rank IC above 0.04 or positive return frequency drops below 52% during GDP expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production and consumer prices signals supply-constrained inflationary pressure, which forces markets to price in tighter financial conditions and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change > 0.5% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3%
failure_condition: The dual-acceleration signal yields positive returns in greater than 55% of triggered cases or fails to maintain statistical significance after adjusting for Federal Reserve forward guidance releases.
